sutta: the buddha said monks when a raja citadel in the frontiers is well provided with the 7 requisites of a fortress and can obtain at will easily and without trouble the 4 kinds of supplies it is said to be 1 that cannot be undone by outside 4 or treacherous ally with what 7 requisites of a fortress is it well provided monks there is in a raja citadel in the frontiers the pillar deeply embedded well dunked in immovable and unshakable but this first fortress requisite is the raja citadel in the frontiers well provided for the protection of the inmates and for the warding off of outsiders monks there is the mode both deep and wide with this second requisite is it well provided there is the road going round the citadel but this third requisite is it well provided there is the great armory of spear and sword with this fourth requisite is it well provided there is the large body of troops stationed in the citadel that is to say mahouts horsemen charioters bowmen standard bearers billeting officers soldiers of the supply corps noted raja's sons storm troops men as brave as mighty nagas valiants warriors in armor and home born slaves with this fifth requisite is it well provided there is the gatekeeper clever intelligent wise who refuses entrance to the unknown but admits those he knows with this sixth requisite is it well provided then monks there is in the rajas citadel in the frontiers the rampart both high and wide covered with a court of plaster with this seventh requisite is the raja citadel well provided for the protection of the inmates and for the warding of of outsiders these 7 fortress requisites is it well provided and what are the 4 kinds of supplies it can obtain at will easily and without trouble there are in the raja citadel in the frontiers great stores of grass wood and water for the convenience of the inmates for their comfort for the well-being and for the warding off of outsiders there are great stores of rice and corn for the convenience of the inmates there are great straws of sesame beans wedges of order and cereals for the convenience of the inmates then monks there are in the rajya citadel in the frontiers great stores of medicines that is to say ghee fresh butter oil honey sugar and salt for the convenience of the inmates for their comfort for their well-being and for the warding of of outsiders these are the 4 kinds of supplies it can obtain at will easily and without trouble verily monks when a raja citadel in the frontiers is well provided with these 7 requisites and can obtain it will easily and without trouble these 4 kinds of supplies it is said to be 1 which cannot be undone by outside 4 or treacherous ally in just the same way monks when an aryan disciple is possessed of 7 excellent things and can obtain at will easily and without trouble 4 janas of the higher mind bringing comfort here now the aryan disciple is said to be 1 who cannot be undone by mara and done by the evil 1 of what 7 excellent things is he possessed just as a raja citadel in the frontiers has the pillar deeply embedded well done in immovable and unshakeable for the protection of the inmates and for the warding off of outsiders even so amongst an aryan disciple has faith he believes in the tathagata's awakening of the truth he is the exalted 1 arahant complete with knowledge and practice well gone 1 world no incomparable team of tamerable men teacher of devas and men exalted 1 with faith as a pillar the aryan disciple abandons unwholesome states and makes wholesome states become he abandons what is blameworthy he makes blamelessness become he bears himself in pureness with this first excellent thing as he possessed just as the citadel as has a moat both deep and white for the protection of the inmates even so monks an aryan disciple is conscientious he is ashamed of misconducting himself indeed word and thought ashamed of falling into evil and unwholesome states with conscientiousness as a mode the aryan disciple abandons unwholesome states and makes wholesome states become of this second excellent thing is he possessed just as the citadel has a road going around it both high and wide for the protection of the inmates even so amongst an aryan disciple has remorse he feels remorse for misconduct indeed word and thought he feels remorse having fallen into evil and unwholesome states with remorse as an encircling road the aryan disciple abandons unwholesome states and makes makes wholesome states become of this third excellent thing as he possessed just as the citadel has a great armory of spear and sword for the protection of the inmates even so monks an aryan disciple has heard much and there is a retaining a storing of things heard those dharmas lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle and lovely in the end which set forth in meaning and detail the holy life wholly fulfilled perfectly pure even those are learned by him resolved upon made familiar by speech pondered over in mind well penetrated by right view with learning as an armory the aryan disciple abandons unwholesome states and makes wholesome states become of this fourth excellent thing as he possessed just as the citadel has a large body of troops stationed therein that is to say mahood's cavalry charioters bowman and so forth for the protection of the inmates even so monks and aryan disciple abides stirred in energy or with sustained energy to be rid of wholesome states to follow wholesome states steadfast firm in advance he lays not aside the burden of wholesome states with energy as an armed force the aryan disciple abandons and wholesome states and makes wholesome states become of this fifth excellent thing is he possessed just as the citadel has a gatekeeper clever intelligent and wise who refuses entrance to the unknown but admits those he knows for the protection of the inmates even so amongst an aryan disciple has mindfulness as in and and is endowed in the highest degree with mindfulness and awareness he remembers and calls to mind things done and said long ago with mindfulness as a gatekeeper the aryan disciple abandons and wholesome states and makes wholesome states become of these 6 excellent things he possessed just as the raja citadel in the frontiers has a rampant both high and wide covered with a coat of plaster for the protection of the inmates and for the warding of of outsiders even so amongst the aryan disciple has wisdom endowed is he with wisdom as to the nature of a rising association with aryan penetration of the way to the utter destruction of ill with wisdom as a court of plaster the aryan disciple abandons unwholesome states he makes wholesome states become he abandons what is blameworthy he makes blamelessness become he bears himself in pureness of this seventh excellent thing as he possessed of these 7 excellent things as he possessed what 4 jaanas of the higher mind bringing comfort here now can he obtain and will easily and without trouble just as in a raja citadel in the frontiers there are great straws of grass wood and water for the convenience of the inmates for their comfort for their well-being and for the warding off of outsiders even so amongst an aryan disciple aloo from sen's desires aloo from unwholesome states enters and abides in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of solitude for his own convenience for his own comfort for his own well-being and for fairing to the cool just as in the citadel there are great stores of rice and corn for the convenience of the inmates even so amongst an aryan disciple with distilling of applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second jhana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration for his own convenience and for faring to the cool just as the citadel has great straws of sesame beans watchers and other cereals for the convenience of the inmates even so monks and aryan disciples with the fading away of delight abides in equanimity and mindful and aware enters and abides in the third jana experiencing pleasure with the body on account of which the orange declare he that is equanimous and mindful has a joyful abiding for his own convenience and for faring to the cool just as in the raja citadel in the frontiers there are great stores of medicines that is to say ghee fresh butter oil honey sugar and salt for the convenience of the inmates for their comfort for their well-being and for the warding off of outsiders even so amongst an aryan disciple with the abandoning of bodily pleasure and the previous abandoning of bodily pain by the fading away of joy and grief enters and abides in the fourth jana which is the complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity and has neither pain nor pleasure for his own convenience for his own comfort for his well-being and for fairing to the cool these are the 4 jaanas of the higher mind bringing comfort here and now he can obtain and will easily and without trouble monks when an ardent disciple is possessed of these 7 excellent things and can obtain at will easily and without trouble these 4 janus this area disciple is said to be 1 who cannot be undone by mara and done by the evil 1 that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this suta is about the 7 excellent things and the pali name is the 7 saddam the 7 saddam this is 1 of the things to acquire on the spiritual path or the holy path there is a name for the buddha which is called vijaya charana sampanu complete with knowledge and conduct or practice the word charana can be translated as conduct or practice it is the steps along the holy path and the servants are dhamma 1 of the things to be acquired along that holy path what are these 7 things the first 1 is having faith that the buddha is the enlightened 1 and the second 1 is conscientiousness or conscience like having a conscience to be ashamed of doing something wrong third 1 is o tapa o tapa is a sense of remorse a sense of guilt if you have done wrong the fourth 1 is much hearing of the dhamma in other words much knowledge of the dhamma the dhamma refers to the discourses of the buddha the words of the buddha and [Music] the fifth 1 is stirred in energy or having sustained energy to get rid of unwholesome states and to cultivate wholesome states of mind yeah the sixth is sakti sati is mindfulness is generally translated as mindfulness but you have to keep in mind the meaning of sati here as in other sutas the definition of sati is given as he remembers and calls to mind things done and said long ago so sati is remembering what is it that we have to remember we have to remember in this case we have to remember that the body and the mind is not i it's not mind it's not the self this is mentioned in the uh sanguita nikaya the samyutta on the 5 kandasa 5 things that we take to be the cell which are also body and mind so that's the meaning of mindfulness here it's not general mindfulness because general mindfulness is not to do with buddhist practice like buddhist practice its own is a specific mindfulness mindfulness of our mind and mindfulness of our body activities and also the nature of the mind and the nature of the body if you roam out into the other 5 sense doors in the world of seeing hearing smelling taste and touch that is what the buddha calls mara's playground and there are hooks mara is always trying to beat us and if you are not careful when you get caught by mara that means nice sights nice sounds nice smells nice taste and pleasant touch so that's the mindfulness the seventh 1 is panya's wisdom wisdom here refers to wisdom as to the nature of arising and cessation of all things in the world because all things in the world have the nature to arise and to pass away and that is if you know the nature of the rising and cessation of all things in the world and that is wisdom so these are the 7 excellent things that person on the holy path should cultivate and in addition to that if he can attain the 4 janus of the higher mind then um this this this aryan disciple is said to be 1 who cannot be undone by mara cannot be undone by the evil 1 um so you see here just now there was a description of the force states of jannah the first 1 aloof from sen's desires aloo from unwholesome states he enters and abides in the first jhana which is accompanied by applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of solitude so you see the first jhana is a statement which is aloof from sense desires sense desires is 1 of the 5 hindrances that obstruct wisdom that obstruct us from seeing things as they really are so the first statement the first jhana is is a state now which is allu from sensual desires it is also aloof from unwholesome states so the state of uh jhana the first jana is a very wholesome state has nothing to do with unwholesome states because here it is defined as being aloof from unwholesome states and in fact it is a state of intense mindfulness if you don't have intense mindfulness you cannot enter into the first jhana and some people have the wrong idea that jhana states of unmindfulness that is totally untrue the second jhana is described as with the stealing of applied and sustained thought he enters and abides in the second jana which has internal self-confidence and singleness of mind without applied and sustained thought with delight and pleasure born of concentration this second jhana is a strong state of concentration so strong that the applied and sustained thought cannot arise no thoughts can arise because it is extremely strong state of concentration and because of that strong state of concentration when a person attains his second genre he has internal self-confidence he is quite confident in that of that concentration the third 1 with the fading away of delight abides in equanimity and mindful and aware enters and abides in the third jana so you see to enter the third jana this person must be mindful and aware he enters and abides in the third jana experiencing pleasure with the body on the count of which the aryans declare he that is equanimous and mindful has a joyful abiding so it's very clear from here also that this third jhana statement is a very mindful and equanimous state in the fourth jana with the abandoning of bodily pleasure and the previous abandoning of bodily pain by the fading away of joy and grief he enters and abides in the fourth jhana which is the complete purity of mindfulness and equanimity and has neither pain nor pleasure so you see this fourth jana the mindfulness is completely purified at the fourth jhana so you cannot be more mindful than at the fourth ghana that's why all these states of jannah are in 10 states of mindfulness definitely uh very mindful states are not unmindful states so if a person possesses these 7 saddam excellent things and the 4 janas mara has no way to bring him down has no way to trap him because he's beyond the reach of mara and a person and enters the janazah and then when he comes out of it the mind is the hindrances are abandoned you know it's quite clear from the previous uttar we read now about sunita the uh external ascetic who cultivated metta and attained the janus about the buddha said that even though he had not rooted out the hindrances but the buddha said he was completely free of lustful passions that means he was completely free of the hindrances it's just like tall grass you know la lang you cut it down even though the roots are not abandoned they do not hinder you anymore they do not the hindrances are not there to hinder you at all so these are the 7 saddam and the 4 chanas now we come to the next 7 point sorry
